task: I’m trying to decide whether to buy a used truck I found, but I don’t want to jump in and regret it later. Can you help me make a simple go/no-go checklist for what I should verify before I spend the money?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple **go / no-go used truck checklist** you can use before buying. I kept it practical and focused on the stuff that can turn into expensive regret.

## Used Truck Go / No-Go Checklist

### 1) Paperwork first
- [ ] Title is clean and matches the seller
- [ ] VIN on title matches VIN on truck
- [ ] No obvious lien / loan problem
- [ ] Service records available
- [ ] Odometer reading makes sense for age/condition
- [ ] No salvage, flood, rebuilt, or branded title unless you’re intentionally buying that

**No-go:** title problems, VIN mismatch, salvage/flood history you didn’t plan for.

---

### 2) Cold start check
- [ ] Truck starts cold without struggling
- [ ] No loud knocking, rattling, or smoke that hangs around
- [ ] Idle is steady
- [ ] Check-engine light comes on with key on, then goes out after start
- [ ] No warning lights stay on

**No-go:** hard start, bad noises, smoke, or warning lights that won’t clear.

---

### 3) Under the hood
- [ ] Oil level and condition look reasonable
- [ ] Coolant level looks right, no oily sludge
- [ ] No fresh leaks under engine, radiator, hoses, or around transmission
- [ ] Belts and hoses aren’t cracked, swollen, or soft
- [ ] Battery terminals aren’t badly corroded
- [ ] No burnt smell or obvious wiring damage

**No-go:** overheating signs, major leaks, or obvious neglected maintenance.

---

### 4) Transmission and drivetrain
- [ ] Shifts smoothly through all gears
- [ ] No slipping, banging, or delayed engagement
- [ ] 4WD engages and disengages properly if equipped
- [ ] No clunks, grinding, or vibration under load
- [ ] No wet seals at axles, transfer case, or driveshaft area

**No-go:** transmission slip, 4WD problems, or drivetrain noise that suggests expensive repair.

---

### 5) Brakes and steering
- [ ] Brake pedal feels firm
- [ ] Truck stops straight
- [ ] No grinding, pulsing, or pulling
- [ ] Steering wheel isn’t loose or wandering
- [ ] No clunks over bumps or when turning

**No-go:** weak brakes, pulling, major steering play, or suspension noise.

---

### 6) Tires and frame/body
- [ ] Tires have even wear and enough tread
- [ ] No dry rot, bulges, or mismatched junk tires
- [ ] Spare is present if it matters to you
- [ ] Frame looks straight
- [ ] No heavy rust, especially on frame, cab mounts, suspension points, brake lines, or bed supports
- [ ] Doors, hood, and tailgate line up reasonably well

**No-go:** frame rust, crash damage, or uneven tire wear that points to bigger problems.

---

### 7) Test drive
- [ ] Drives straight
- [ ] No overheating
- [ ] No odd smells
- [ ] No shaking at speed
- [ ] No warning lights after driving
- [ ] AC, heater, windows, locks, wipers, lights all work

**No-go:** overheating, vibration, electrical issues, or anything that changes how it drives.

---

## Quick decision rule
### Buy only if:
- title is clean
- truck starts cold and runs clean
- no major leaks or warning lights
- transmission/brakes/steering feel solid
- rust is minor
- test drive is boring

### Walk away if:
- title is sketchy
- it overheats
- transmission slips or bangs
- frame rust is serious
- it has multiple warning lights
- seller won’t let you inspect it cold or test drive it properly

## Best next step
If it passes this checklist, I’d still strongly suggest:
- [ ] pre-purchase inspection by a mechanic
- [ ] scan for codes
- [ ] check the truck cold, not after it’s been warmed up

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page printable checklist** with checkboxes and a simple **go / no-go box at the bottom**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. Straightforward and usable. The no-go items are the right ones, especially title, cold start, overheating, transmission slip, and frame rust. That’s the stuff that gets expensive fast.

I’d print it and add one more line for maintenance proof — oil changes, coolant service, transmission service if they have it. If the seller can’t show anything and the truck’s already got miles on it, that’s not a dealbreaker by itself, but it does move me toward walking away or paying less.

So as-is, solid checklist. Nothing fancy, just the right basics.
